_note: 2026-07-22 batch (batch 10). This round's rejects were informative: the Masa 'Israel Wine Experience' at Ohalo College could not be traced to any live official page (a similarly-named tourism company, israelwinexp.com, is unrelated); Sderot Media Center's international internship is likely real (found via search) but every page describing it returned blank/empty content to WebFetch (JS-rendered), so it wasn't included without directly-verified content; the Tikvah Fund's Beren Summer Fellowship is US-based (New York), out of scope; 'Olami Launch' turned out to be a scholarship/facilitation umbrella that funds students into yeshivot and seminaries already in this DB (Baer Miriam, Sharfman's, MMY, Heichal Yerushalayim, Netiv Aryeh, etc. -- all cross-checked and already listed), not a standalone program itself, so it was not added as its own entry. Israel's licensed tour guide course requires Israeli citizenship or permanent residency, excluding this wiki's overseas-diaspora audience. The Tikvah Israel Fellowship itself (initially researched as a candidate) turned out to already be in the DB -- caught by the pre-import slug-collision check, not added here.

programs.name: Olami JHealth Gap Year
programs.description: A medical-track gap year combining a week-long orientation with two three-month internship rotations (clinical, then lab research) at Israeli hospitals and research institutions such as Hadassah Ein Kerem, Shaare Zedek, and the Ministry of Health, alongside Jewish medical ethics study.
programs.durationText: ~7 months (1-week orientation + 3 months clinical + 3 months lab internship)
programs.cost: ~$6,900 (Masa grants available); $500 non-refundable deposit, remainder in installments
programs.adminNote: The specific dates on the fetched program page (Oct 2022-May 2023) appear stale, but a follow-up search confirmed the program structure (week orientation + 3-month clinical + 3-month lab rounds at named Israeli hospitals) is still being actively described and recruited for via a live application form and jhealth@olami.org contact -- worth confirming exact current-cohort dates before outreach. Distinct from the already-listed 'Medical School for International Health (MSIH)' and 'Technion American Medical School (TeAMS)' -- this is a gap-year internship track, not a full medical degree program.
